title: Service Health Score Trending
spl:
index=itsi_summary is_service_in_maintenance=0
| timechart span=1h avg(health_score) by service_name
description: Service health scores provide a single-pane view of business service status. Trending enables SLA reporting and proactive management.
value: Service health scores provide a single-pane view of business service status. Trending enables SLA reporting and proactive management.
implementation: Configure ITSI services with KPIs mapped to business services. Track health scores over time. Alert on score degradation. Use for SLA reporting and executive dashboards. Configure Glass Tables for NOC display.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `itsi_summary` index.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure ITSI services with KPIs mapped to business services. Track health scores over time. Alert on score degradation. Use for SLA reporting and executive dashboards. Configure Glass Tables for NOC display.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsi_summary is_service_in_maintenance=0
| timechart span=1h avg(health_score) by service_name
```

Understanding this SPL

**Service Health Score Trending** — Service health scores provide a single-pane view of business service status. Trending enables SLA reporting and proactive management.

Documented **Data sources**: `itsi_summary` index. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsi_summary.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsi_summary. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets with a separate series **by service_name** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Service Analyzer (ITSI native), Glass Table, Line chart (health trend), Status grid.
visualization: Service Analyzer (ITSI native), Glass Table, Line chart (health trend), Status grid.
